CVE-2013-10075.title: Apache::Session versions through 1.94 for Perl re-creates deleted ...
CVE-2013-10075.description:
Apache::Session versions through 1.94 for Perl re-creates deleted sessions.

The session stores Apache::Session::Store::File and Apache::Session::Store::DB_File will create a session that does not exist.  This can lead to sessions being revived, potentially with data that was to be deleted.

CVE-2026-4176.title: Perl versions from 5.9.4 before 5.40.4-RC1, from 5.41.0 before 5. ...
CVE-2026-4176.description:
Perl versions from 5.9.4 before 5.40.4-RC1, from 5.41.0 before 5.42.2-RC1, from 5.43.0 before 5.43.9 contain a vulnerable version of Compress::Raw::Zlib.

Compress::Raw::Zlib is included in the Perl package as a dual-life core module, and is vulnerable to CVE-2026-3381 due to a vendored version of zlib which has several vulnerabilities, including CVE-2026-27171. The bundled Compress::Raw::Zlib was updated to version 2.221 in Perl blead commit c75ae9cc164205e1b6d6dbd57bd2c65c8593fe94.

CVE-2026-45190.title: Net::CIDR::Lite versions before 0.24 for Perl does not properly v ...
CVE-2026-45190.description:
Net::CIDR::Lite versions before 0.24 for Perl does not properly validate IP address and CIDR mask inputs, which may allow IP ACL bypass.

Inputs containing a trailing newline or non-ASCII digit characters pass the validators but are then re-encoded by the parser to a different address than the input string spelled. find() and bin_find() can match or miss addresses as a result.

Example:

  my $cidr = Net::CIDR::Lite->new();
  $cidr->add("::1\n/128");
  $cidr->find("::1a");  # incorrectly returns true

See also CVE-2026-45191.

CVE-2026-6659.title: Crypt::PasswdMD5 versions through 1.42 for Perl generates insecur ...
CVE-2026-6659.description:
Crypt::PasswdMD5 versions through 1.42 for Perl generates insecure random values for salts.

The built-in rand function is predictable, and unsuitable for cryptography.

CVE-2026-8376.title: Perl versions through 5.43.10 have a heap buffer overflow when co ...
CVE-2026-8376.description:
Perl versions through 5.43.10 have a heap buffer overflow when compiling regular expressions with a repeated fixed string on 32-bit builds.

Perl_study_chunk in regcomp_study.c checked the size of the joined substring buffer in characters rather than bytes. For a quantified fixed substring with a large minimum count, the byte length mincount * l could overflow SSize_t, producing an undersized SvGROW allocation; the subsequent copy writes past the end of the buffer.

A caller that compiles an attacker-controlled regular expression on a 32-bit perl build triggers a heap buffer overflow at compile time.
